findings.description: Cover page logo/image appears slightly different in sizing but is present and correctly placed
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC Coastal Recreational Fishing License logo centered on the cover page; HTML shows the same logo but slightly smaller in relative size

findings.description: Marine Resources Fund Balance table in PDF is a traditional spreadsheet-style table with clear grid lines, while HTML renders it as a styled table with different visual structure but same data
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a compact spreadsheet with all rows visible including Subtotal (Expense) and Total Net rows; HTML page 2 shows the same table but split across pages with the bottom rows (Subtotal Expense, Total Net) separated

findings.description: Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance table - the Subtotal (Expense) values show parenthetical notation in PDF but dollar sign with parentheses in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows $(362,462), HTML shows $ (362,462) with slightly different spacing

findings.description: Page 3 PDF contains Marine Resources Fund Balance, Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance, Projects Approved for Funding, and Summary sections all on one page. HTML splits these across multiple pages/scrollable sections
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 is a dense single page with all fund information; HTML splits this into separate sections across pages 2-6

findings.description: Endowment Fund Balance table - FY2014 Agent Commission value shows slight discrepancy between versions
findings.evidence: PDF shows $29,543 for FY2014 Agent Commission; HTML shows $29,543 - values match but one version also shows $520,330 for Subtotal Gross FY2014 while the other shows $520,330

findings.description: County-level license sales tables maintain structure but ranking columns and data alignment differ slightly in visual presentation
findings.evidence: PDF tables have tight grid formatting; HTML tables use more generous spacing but contain the same data

findings.description: The county sales tables in PDF are marked as '(Continued)' across pages; HTML preserves this but splits differently across rendered pages
findings.evidence: PDF has clear page breaks with 'Continued' headers; HTML reformats continuation across different break points

findings.description: For Hire Blanket table - 2014 data only shows 6 or fewer passengers row (no More than 6 passengers row) in both versions, which is correct since there were no sales in that category
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show only '6 or fewer passengers' for 2014 with Total Sold of 2

findings.description: Sales outlet table and DMF Sales Office table maintain correct data but percentage formatting differs slightly
findings.evidence: PDF shows percentages like '77.47' while HTML shows '77.47' - consistent formatting

findings.description: Two h1 headings used instead of one. The document title is split into two h1 elements.
findings.evidence:
HTML has '

Coastal Recreational Fishing License

' and '

Sales Update

' as separate h1 tags. Should be a single h1 or h1+h2 combination.

findings.description: In the Wilmington DMF office row for 2010, the percentage is shown as 15.53 in the HTML but the PDF shows it as 12.95 (calculated from 331/2555). Actually upon re-reading, the PDF page 20 shows Wilmington 2010 as 331 and 15.53 for % Sold - but wait, 331/2555 = 12.95%. However the PDF itself shows 15.53. This appears to be reproduced faithfully from the source.
In the Marine Resources Fund Balance table, the Wilmington 2010 Manteo percentage discrepancy is actually in the source PDF. The HTML faithfully reproduces the PDF values. No actual error found here.
The MEXICO entry in the country table for 2014 shows 'No. Sold' as 0 but has Rank 4 in the HTML. The PDF shows the same (0 sold, rank 4). This is faithfully reproduced from the source.
findings.evidence: PDF page 13: MEXICO 2014 No. Sold = 0, Rank = 4; HTML matches.

findings.description: The document lacks intermediate heading levels. Section titles like 'Marine Resources Fund Balance' and 'Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance' are rendered as bold paragraphs rather than proper headings (h2/h3), which doesn't reflect the document's hierarchical structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF has distinct section headers 'Marine Resources Fund Balance', 'Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance', 'Projects Approved for Funding...', 'SUMMARY' which are rendered as

in HTML instead of heading tags.
